channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Addressing Gender and Social Inequities in India’s Fight Against Drug-Resistant Tuberculosis
item.description: The article by Ashna Ashesh, published on Scroll.in for World Tuberculosis Day, discusses the urgent need for gender-responsive approaches in the prevention and treatment of drug-resistant tuberculosi...

item.title: The Role of Faith Leaders in Advancing Middle East Peace
item.description: Bejani Chauke argues that resolving conflicts in the Middle East requires more than just diplomats and military strategies. The humanitarian crises in Gaza, Lebanon and Iran show that political negoti...

item.title: DIT partners with CEBOT to commercialise innovations and boost technology in Tanzania
item.description: The Dar es Salaam Institute of Technology (DIT) has officially signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the United States-based Council Exchange Board of Trade (CEBOT) aimed at commercialising ...

item.title: UNICEF and partners push campaign to vaccinate 1.5 million Jigawa children against polio
item.description: UNICEF don call on parents, media people, and community leaders for Jigawa State make dem join hand to remove polio totally from the area. The call happen for one-day Mass Mobilisation Dialogue wey UN...

item.title: OPP Seizes Switchblade and Drugs Following Ankle Monitor Alert
item.description: The Ontario Provincial Police (OPP) reported a recent incident in Northern Ontario where an ankle monitor alert led to the seizure of a switchblade knife and illegal drugs. The alert, triggered by a m...

item.title: Viktor Orbán’s international alliances may undermine his domestic support in upcoming Hungarian elections
item.description: Viktor Orbán, Hungary’s prime minister, has become a prominent figure within the international authoritarian right, admired by leaders such as Donald Trump, Benjamin Netanyahu, Giorgia Meloni, and Mar...

item.title: Federal Government Requests FSIN Repay $28.7M After Financial Review
item.description: The federal government is requesting that the Federation of Sovereign Indigenous Nations (FSIN) repay over $28.7 million after a forensic audit raised concerns about the use of federal funding between...

item.title: DOE investigates gas station closures amid oil crisis and possible hoarding
item.description: The Department of Energy (DOE) has launched an investigation into the reported shutdown of more than 400 fuel retail stations across the Philippines to determine whether hoarding or profiteering is in...

item.title: The importance of guiding young Africans with strong sociopolitical values
item.description: Dis one article dey raise a big issue about young Africans and di values wey dem dey inherit from di older generations. Wit more than 70 percent of di continent under 30, di question of sociopolitical...

item.title: VP Sara Duterte yet to confirm attendance at House impeachment hearing
item.description: The House Committee on Justice announced that it has not yet received any indication from the camp of Vice President Sara Duterte about whether she will attend the impeachment hearing scheduled for Ma...

item.title: Trump delays Iran ultimatum amid missile strikes and market reactions
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East escalated as Iranian missile attacks targeted multiple cities in Israel, including Tel Aviv and Nesher, causing damage to buildings, vehicles, and wounding civilians. Israe...

item.title: Colombian Air Force C-130 crash kills 66, leaves four missing
item.description: At least 66 people have died and four remain missing following a Colombian Air Force C-130 Hercules plane crash in the southern Amazon region, near the border with Peru. The aircraft, carrying 128 pas...

item.title: New Jersey Morgue Employee Terminated for Sharing Confidential Information About Gaudreau Brothers’ Deaths
item.description: A New Jersey Civil Service Commission has upheld the firing of a morgue worker who leaked confidential details regarding the deaths of NHL players Johnny Gaudreau and his brother Matthew Gaudreau, who...

item.title: Female MNAs actively contribute to Pakistan’s national policy matters
item.description: During the second parliamentary year of the 16th National Assembly, female MNAs in Pakistan have shown remarkable legislative initiative by submitting 1,206 agenda items. Analysis by the Free and Fair...

item.title: Jerry Pinto remembers R. Parthasarathy and the quiet fading of an Indian English poetry pioneer
item.description: In this tribute, writer and poet Jerry Pinto reflects on the life, work and slow disappearance of R. Parthasarathy, the Tamil‑born poet and translator who passed away on March 7, 2026, in Saratoga Spr...

item.title: HYBE India Launches Auditions Across Major Cities to Discover Local Music Talent
item.description: HYBE, the global entertainment company behind the internationally acclaimed K-pop group BTS, has officially announced auditions across India through its local division, HYBE India. These auditions aim...

item.title: Soweto Residents Demand Affordable Electricity and Consultation from NERSA
item.description: Hundreds of residents from Soweto marched to the National Energy Regulator of South Africa (NERSA) offices in Pretoria on Monday, 23 March 2026, demanding a flat rate electricity tariff of R350 for po...

item.title: Nigeria Wheelchair Basketball Team Departs for Angola Commonwealth Games Qualifiers
item.description: The Nigeria Wheelchair Basketball Team don leave Naija go Angola to participate for the upcoming Commonwealth Games Qualifiers wey go hold from March 22 to 27, 2026 for Luanda, Angola. Eight athletes ...

item.title: Oil climbs back above $100 per barrel amid ongoing Middle East conflict
item.description: Oil prices moved back above $100 per barrel late on Monday after an earlier dip in trading, reflecting renewed market anxiety over the ongoing war between Iran and a U.S.–Israel alliance. Brent crude,...

item.title: OPP Stops Commercial Truck Exceeding Speed Limit in Northern Ontario
item.description: Ontario Provincial Police (OPP) officers stopped a commercial vehicle in Northern Ontario after detecting it travelling at 108 km/h in a 60 km/h zone, raising concerns about its roadworthiness. Author...
